title: RVA23, XiangShan, openEuler — CPU IP team's blueprint for OS validation

slides.title:
From a CPU IP team's perspective:
the practice of OS validation
slides.subtitle: RVA23 · XiangShan · openEuler 24.03 LTS

slides.kicker: THE PROFILE THAT CHANGED THE CONVERSATION
slides.title: RVA23 — ratified October 2024
slides.label: Mandatory

sideCard.label: WHY IT MATTERS
sideCard.body: First time the kernel can target RVA23 as a dependable high-performance baseline — compile to it, not around it. One ISA profile. One compile target. One cross-vendor validation baseline.

slides.title: Linux 7.0 — RVA23 mandatory coverage at 100%
slides.subtitle: v7.0-rc1 released 2026-02-22 · rva23u64 / rva23s64 mandatory all in mainline

cards.label: PRIMARY DRIVER
cards.body: SpacemiT K3 — first commercial RVA23 SoC, taped out 2025-12. Missing extensions filled during upstream review.

cards.label: EXTENSIONS ADDED
cards.body: Sha- (Hypervisor), Ss- (Supervisor), Zicc- (cache coherence), Za64rs (atomic reservation set). Merged after v2/v3/v4 iterations.

cards.label: supm DEBATE
cards.body: Heinrich Schuchardt (Canonical) flagged supm missing from ISA string. Consensus: describe in dt-bindings, synthesize from Smnpm/Ssnpm.

cards.label: NEXT
cards.body: v7.1 / v7.2: CPU feature parsing exposed via cpuinfo and hwprobe to userspace; rva23u64/s64 as a dedicated base ISA.

slides.note: Cross-vendor, cross-SoC expressive completeness — the prerequisite for treating one Profile as the validation starting point.

slides.title: Kernel constant. Workload variant.
slides.note: In CPU validation, the build system is a first-order correctness problem — not an engineering convenience.

layers.label: LAYER 1 · IMMUTABLE
layers.name: Firmware + kernel + DTB
layers.sub: Revision-managed. Byte-identical across an entire test campaign.

layers.label: LAYER 2 · TEST IMAGE
layers.name: Workload + datasets + task-runner.sh
layers.sub: Produced as cpio (initrd) and ext4 (block device). Varies with test intent.

layers.label: LAYER 3 · INTENT
layers.name: Kernel cmdline
layers.sub: Read by task-runner.sh at boot. Decides which permutation runs.

companion.label: PROPERTY
companion.body: Storage-media agnostic — NVDIMM, NVMe, USB. Boot params decide.

companion.label: PROPERTY
companion.body: One build. Test image rebuilt only when workload campaign changes.

companion.label: PROPERTY
companion.body: Zero ambiguity — kernel-changed-between-runs is not a question we ask.

slides.title: Almost the same as container distribution
One branch. Two memory layouts.
slides.label: PHYSICAL MEMORY MAP
slides.diagram:
0x8000_0000  ┌─ OpenSBI ──────────────────────────────────────┐
             │ Firmware reserve                              │
             ├─ Host kernel (defconfig + xiangshan.config) ──┤
             │ ...                                           │
0x8020_0000  ├─ Host rootfs (< 512 MB)  ─────────────────────┤
             │ task-runner.sh + workloads + OverlayFS        │
0xA000_0000  ├─ Task region (cap 2 GB)  ─────────────────────┤
             │ SPEC datasets, scratch, /tmp uppers           │
0x4_6000_0000├─ Guest rootfs (KVM, virtio-9p shared)  ───────┤
             │ Same task-runner.sh binary, byte-identical    │
             └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

slides.title: Thank you.
slides.subtitle: Questions, critique, collaboration — all welcome.
slides.quote: The free development of each is the condition for the free development of all.
slides.quoteAttribution: Marx / Engels · Manifesto of the Communist Party
